item.title: Eldorado Gold Wins Investor Support to Buy Copper-Focused Foran
item.description: Eldorado Gold Corp.’s C$3.8 billion ($2.7 billion) bid to buy Foran Mining Corp. won shareholder support, advancing plans to create a larger Canadian gold-and-copper miner after the company overcame an activist campaign that had cast doubt on the deal’s prospects.

item.title: IMF Chief Warns World Is Ill-Equipped to Counter Iran War Risks
item.description: The International Monetary Fund is poised to cut its forecasts for global growth as a result of the war in Iran, and sees danger in a world economy that’s ill-equipped to respond to shocks, the Fund’s chief said in an interview Tuesday.

item.title: Key Real-World Oil Price Soars to Highest Level on Record
item.description: The world’s most important price for real-world oil barrels soared to its highest level on record, the latest sign that global crude markets are pricing increasingly scarce supply as a result of the disruption from the Iran war.

item.title: Pimco Weighs $14 Billion Debt Deal for Oracle Data Center
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is in talks with Bank of America Corp. to help provide roughly $14 billion of debt financing to build a massive Oracle Corp. data center in Michigan, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Anthropic Lets Apple, Amazon Test More Powerful Mythos AI Model
item.description: Anthropic PBC is letting tech firms access a more powerful, unreleased artificial intelligence model to help prepare for possible cyberattacks that might result from the company making the advanced AI system more widely available.

item.title: FDIC Lays Out Guidelines for Institutions Issuing Stablecoins
item.description: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corp., which backs deposits at thousands of US banks, is laying out its guidelines for how those institutions and their fintech subsidiaries can use stablecoins as digital currencies become more widely accepted in the financial system.

item.title: US Emergency Oil Heads to Distant Peru With Decades-Old Energy Flows Upended
item.description: Oil from the US emergency reserve is heading to unlikely destinations as the biggest crude-market disruption in history upends decades-old global energy flows.

item.title: Sony Pictures Entertainment Cuts Hundreds of Jobs in TV, Film
item.description: Sony Pictures Entertainment is restructuring its business to better position the company for growth, leading to job cuts across its film, TV and corporate divisions in the coming months.

item.title: Private-Credit Worry Sparks Slide in Booming Part of Muni Market
item.description: The concerns building in the private-credit industry are starting to inflict pain in a rapidly growing segment of the US municipal debt market.

item.title: Derivatives Head Mackay Is Latest to Leave Metals Trader IXM
item.description: Tom Mackay, head of derivatives at IXM Holding SA, is leaving the metals trading house — the latest of several senior departures in recent months — according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Apple’s Foldable iPhone Remains on Track for September Debut
item.description: Apple Inc.’s first foldable phone is on track to arrive during the company’s normal iPhone launch period later this year, people with knowledge of the matter said, rebutting concerns about major manufacturing snags.

item.title: Sixth Street Buys First UK Team With Sunderland AFC Women Stake
item.description: Sixth Street, the global investment firm, has made its first foray into UK football by agreeing a deal to buy a majority stake in Sunderland AFC Women, the second-tier English football team.

item.title: France to Fast-Track Law to Protect Its Struggling Farmers
item.description: France is moving to fast-track emergency legislation to protect its farmers from rising imports as Europe’s top agricultural producer faces a collapse in its food trade balance.

item.title: Anthropic Poaches Microsoft Executive to Lead Infrastructure
item.description: Anthropic PBC has hired a senior leader from Microsoft Corp. to lead its push to establish the infrastructure needed to support growing adoption of its artificial intelligence services.

item.title: MidEast Crude Output Seen Dropping by 9 Million Barrels a Day
item.description: More than 9 million barrels a day of oil production from key Middle Eastern countries are expected to be shut in during April, according to estimates from the US government, as the war in Iran upends global energy markets.

item.title: Billions Flow Out of BlackRock’s India ETF as Iran War Escalates
item.description: Investors are accelerating their flight from the largest US-listed exchange-traded fund tracking Indian equities, as concerns mount over the impact of the global energy crisis on the Asian economy.

item.title: Why Trump Is Eyeing Iran’s Kharg Island Oil Export Hub
item.description: Iran’s oil exports are almost completely dependent on a small outpost in the Persian Gulf: Kharg Island.

item.title: Key Oil Pricing Window Sees Slew of Bids as War Hits Supply
item.description: A key North Sea oil-pricing period saw 12 unanswered bids for cargoes and surging prices, the latest sign of tightness in global crude markets as the Iran war hits global output.

item.title: George Soros’ Firm Invests in Progressive MeidasTouch Network
item.description: The MeidasTouch Network has taken off on YouTube as a top podcast with 300 million views a month

item.title: Ferraris, Advil y chips de IA: 10 compañías clave a seguir
item.description: Analistas de Bloomberg Intelligence han examinado minuciosamente miles de empresas que cotizan en bolsa para destacar unas pocas que merecen la atención de los inversionistas.

item.title: Gamma Communications Is Said to Explore Options Including Sale
item.description: Gamma Communications Plc is exploring options including a potential sale of the business, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Colombia Oil Workers Drop Strike Threat After Ecopetrol CEO Exit
item.description: Colombia’s largest oil workers union dropped a strike threat after Ecopetrol SA’s chief executive officer was removed following a board decision.

item.title: Ukraine Says It Hit Russia’s Key Baltic Oil Port of Ust-Luga
item.description: Ukraine said it hit Russia’s key Baltic port of Ust-Luga, the latest in a spate of attacks on the country’s oil-export infrastructure as the war between the two nations shows no signs of easing.

item.title: Treasuries Fall Ahead of Auction as Iran War Stokes Oil Gains
item.description: Treasuries fell amid mounting oil prices in the run-up to US President Donald Trump’s late-Tuesday deadline for Iran to agree to terms for ending American attacks.

item.title: Private Credit Exodus Turns Moody’s Outlook on BDCs to Negative
item.description: A swelling wave of redemptions has driven Moody’s Ratings to revise its outlook for private credit investment vehicles to negative, after holding the line at stable for over two years.

item.title: Inflation Expectations Jump With Iran War, Fed Survey Finds
item.description: Near-term inflation expectations jumped in March by the most in a year as consumers anticipated higher gas and food prices with the onset of war in the Middle East, according to a Federal Reserve Bank of New York survey released Tuesday.

item.title: OPEC Output Fell Most in Decades Last Month on War, Survey Shows
item.description: OPEC’s crude oil production plunged by the most in at least four decades in March as conflict in the Middle East throttled exports from key members, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: Zambia-Lobito Copper Rail Link to Cost as Much as $5 Billion
item.description: A railway planned to connect Zambia’s copper mines to global markets via the Lobito port in neighboring Angola will cost as much as $5 billion, with construction set to start his year, according to an environmental study for the project.

item.title: Russia-Linked Hackers Hijack Routers to Steal Passwords, UK Says
item.description: Russian government-linked hackers are compromising popular internet routers to steal passwords for email accounts and other online services, the UK’s National Cyber Security Centre has warned.

item.title: UBS Taps Bond Insurer for Debt Backed by Private Credit Stakes
item.description: UBS Group AG is packaging its stakes in eight private credit funds into debt that’s backed by an insurance company, a financial maneuver that would allow the bank to cash out of the positions without having to unload them directly.
